title: malus transitoria var. centralasiatica
intro: Malus transitoria var. centralasiatica, also known as the Central Asian wild apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Malus transitoria var. centralasiatica include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, which is used to treat various health conditions, and its fruits are also consumed fresh or used to make jams and preserves.
